paired_comparisons.threshold_scope_no_ft: 1a vs 7  → global vs per-layer (both: no ft)
paired_comparisons.fine_tune_value: 7  vs 3  → no ft vs ft=Nep (both: per-layer L1)
paired_comparisons.pruning_schedule: 3  vs 6  → one-shot vs iterative (both: per-layer L1)
paired_comparisons.ft_value_global: 1a vs 1b → ft effect on global-pruned model

1a_unstructured_noft.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
1a_unstructured_noft.method_note: Global L1 unstructured, no fine-tuning. Paired with 7_one_shot (per-layer, no ft) → isolates threshold scope. Paired with 1b_unstructured_ft (same pruning, adds ft) → isolates ft value.

1b_unstructured_ft.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
1b_unstructured_ft.method_note: Global L1 unstructured + 10-epoch fine-tune @ lr=0.001. Paired with 1a (isolates ft value) and 3_magnitude (isolates threshold scope).

2_structured.flops_note: TRUE structured pruning: 30% of internal Bottleneck channels physically removed (conv1+bn1+conv2 per block). conv3, bn3, downsample untouched — residual shapes guaranteed. GFLOPs genuinely reduced; measured by thop on rebuilt model.

3_magnitude.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
3_magnitude.method_note: Per-layer L1 magnitude pruning + 10-epoch fine-tune @ lr=0.001. Han et al. 2015. Central reference method.

5_lottery_ticket.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
5_lottery_ticket.lth_note: LTH — Frankle & Carlin 2019. θ₀ @ SEED=42. θ_T: 50-epoch in-script training (lr=0.1, SGD, ColorJitter). Global magnitude mask @ 50% on Conv2d+Linear weights only. Ticket retrained 50 epochs with frozen mask.

6_iterative.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
6_iterative.method_note: 3-round per-layer L1 iterative pruning. Per-round sparsity: 0.2063 → compounds to 50%. Fine-tune epochs: [4, 3, 3] per round (total=10). Constant LR=0.001 across all rounds. make_permanent() called once after all rounds.

7_one_shot.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
7_one_shot.method_note: Per-layer L1, 50% sparsity, zero fine-tuning. Paired with 1a (global threshold, no ft) → isolates threshold scope. Paired with 3 (same criterion, ft=Nep) → isolates fine-tuning value.

8_movement_pruning.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
8_movement_pruning.method_note: True movement pruning (Sanh et al. 2020). Score = sign(W0) * (W1 - W0). Weights moving toward zero are pruned globally. Mask applied post-training via p.mul_() — no live hook (movement requires ft first).
